picks.reason: Miami starter Ryan Gusto is flagged as a small sample size, which increases projected variance. Although Zack Wheeler is elite, the -205 price tag on Philadelphia offers zero value, and our projected win probability aligns closely with the market.

Andrew Alvarez is flagged as a small sample starter, while Mitch Spence has a highly regression-prone profile. Key bullpen arms for Kansas City are heavily taxed, but the market price on Washington already accounts for these factors, leaving no actionable edge.

This is a premier pitching mismatch between Chase Burns (2.98 SIERA, 24.5% K-BB%) and the highly regression-prone Tobias Myers (5.36 SIERA, 4.9% K-BB%). At -131, the market is severely underpricing Burns' elite underlying metrics and his matchup against a mediocre Mets offense.
This game features an unconfirmed TBD starting pitcher for San Diego. Per strict data integrity guidelines, we must pass on all games with incomplete starting pitcher information.

Shota Imanaga faces a weak Colorado offense (68 wRC+ vs LHP), while Michael Lorenzen faces a competent Cubs squad. However, the heavy market price of -195 on Chicago is fair, leaving no statistical margin to justify a premium unit wager.
This game features an unconfirmed TBD starting pitcher for Minnesota. Per strict data integrity guidelines, we must pass on all games with incomplete starting pitcher information.
Troy Melton and Kai-Wei Teng present very similar baseline projections (4.46 vs 4.38 SIERAs). With the offenses and bullpens relatively balanced, the Houston price of -125 offers no value.

The market is overvaluing Arizona here, as their offense has sputtered to a weak 80 wRC+ against right-handed pitching. Walbert Ureña holds a solid matchup edge against this lineup, while Ryne Nelson is vulnerable against a decent Angels offense.

Jared Jones is a small-sample starter making his profile highly volatile, and Sutter Health Park is an extreme hitter-friendly environment. We lean toward the home team but pass on a premium bet due to the high variance.

Nick Martinez has been highly effective (2.43 ERA) and offers a solid baseline against a tough Dodgers lineup. Eric Lauer is highly vulnerable, making Tampa Bay an intriguing lean at +143, though it falls just short of our 4.0-point edge threshold.

parlay.reason: Both games present highly independent starting pitching mismatches that are undervalued by the market. Combining them compounds our positive expected value without correlation.

best_bet.why_this: Chase Burns (2.98 SIERA) holds an elite pitching advantage over Tobias Myers (5.36 SIERA), creating a massive projection gap at cheap -131 odds.
